observations.recommendation:
We reiterated the previous recommendation that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to exert extra effort to locate the whereabouts of the accountable officers and cause the liquidation of the long outstanding cash advances in compliance with the government existing rules and regulations; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to file a request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts and fund transfers together with supporting documents in order to present fairly the financial statements of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to stop the practice of granting cash advances for operating expenses considering that the LGU has no operating or field units; and
Instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to establish petty cash fund for payment of authorized petty or miscellaneous expenses which cannot be conveniently paid through checks, otherwise, issue checks directly payable to the supplier or creditor.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to prepare the RPCPPE so that minimum data required will be supplied and the functions and purposes of the RCPPE will be attained;
Require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to:
update and complete the Property Cards per category of PPE which are necessary to support the accuracy and reliability of the balances of PPE account in the financial statements;
prepare updated list of accountable officers and update the PAR every three years or every time there is a change of accountable officer; and
coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to initiate compliance with the guidelines and procedures for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts.
Instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records in order to trace, identify and classify the specific PPE items to its appropriate PPE account; and
Require the Inventory Committee to identify and collate all unserviceable properties and the necessary procedures on disposal of unserviceable properties.

We recommended that Management instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor to continuously exhaust all possible means to document the legality of the unregistered parcel of land with tax declarations to facilitate the transfer of ownership in the name of the Municipality to establish absolute ownership and to avoid exposure to third party claims and possible land disputes.
We further recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant, Acting Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor to reconcile their records on Land account and to facilitate the recording of the unrecorded parcels of land in the books with supporting documents that were identified in the name of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record all financial transactions in accordance with the Chart of Accounts prescribed in the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs;
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries on the aforementioned transactions to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements as well as the correct depreciation expense amount; and
Require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to:
reclassify in the RPCLRN the Installation of the Solar Road Lights to its appropriate PPE account;
coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to identify the breakdown of the lumpsum amount on Other Projects and LGU counterpart reported in the RPCLRN; and
prepare and maintain the Road Map.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to trace the JEVs received from the DPWH as her basis in preparing the adjusting journal entry to record the costs of infrastructure projects transferred to the LGU duly supported with the documents received from the DPWH.
The suggested journal entry is as follows:
We also recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation costs for the Road Networks, Buildings and Hospital and Health Centers based on the date the projects were completed and available for use.
We further recommended that Management coordinate with the DPWH to secure copies of the JEVs of the infrastructure projects transferred to LGU for proper recording in the books, in case said received JEVs cannot be located.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to trace the details of the difference arising from the reconciliation of SCBAA and SFPe under income and MOOE amounting to ₱26,419.00 and ₱2,153,854.84, respectively, and to provide adequate disclosures of the presented reconciling items in the reconciliation statement to ensure that relevant information is available to the intended users of the financial statements; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to maintain RAAO and SAAOB, respectively, in support of the actual amounts in the SCBAA and the reconciliation with the amounts presented in the SFPe.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unexpended balance of LDRRMF for capital outlay to the General Fund.
We also recommended that Management ensure that all unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF for capital outlay shall be made continuing in the General Fund books until the projects funded therefore are completed and any savings shall be made available for use in the disaster risk reduction and management activities as provided in the LDRRMFIP subject to the local budgeting process.

We also recommended that Management provide a stockroom for the use of the Acting Municipal Treasurer to keep the supplies and materials purchased including equipment which are to be issued to the requisitioning head of department needing the same.

We recommended that Management require the implementing offices to ensure that the programs of the LGU are adequately planned by incorporating the specific activities needed to be undertaken in the AIP for proper funding thereof in the Annual and Supplemental Budgets of the LGU to avoid the recurrence of the deficiency noted.

Section l5 of RA 9344, and to direct the designated members of the LCPC to
prepare Local Development Plan for Children and Local Investment Plan for
Children, and to maximize the utilization of the allocated LCPC Fund to the
identified PPAs as provided for in DILG MC No. 2012-120 to ensure full
strengthening and implementation of the LCPC for the benefit of intended
beneficiaries.

We recommended that Management follow-up the approval of the
submitted 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan, implement and enforce the
Ecological Solid Waste Management Program by appropriating funds to be
utilized fully to attain the objective embodied in Republic Act 9003 and to avoid
additional cost in the disposal of wastes. We also recommended that
Management ensure the establishment of Material Recovery Facility (MRF) in
every barangay or cluster barangay and formulate a program that will
encourage the constituents on how to reuse, recycle and reduce wastes.

We recommended that management take the following courses of action:
a. Conduct the required annual physical count of the PPE as of December of every year. The actual count may be undertaken any month during the current year, but has to be updated at year-end. This procedure is to be undertaken in order to know the physical existence and condition of the PPE; and to identify, segregate and reclassify unserviceable assets. Reconciliation of the result of the count with the accounting records be made to determine any difference and to effect the necessary adjustments; and pinpoint responsibility/accountability/liability for any missing PPE;
b. Maintain and update subsidiary ledgers, PPE ledger cards, Property Acknowledgement Receipts, and put property tags which are required under MNGAS for proper control, monitoring and identification of the PPE of the Municipality; and
c. Require the use of Inventory Custodian Slip for semi-expendable items to avoid loss, as these are usually small items.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management take the following courses of action:
a. Create an Inventory Committee and require the same to conduct
physical inventory and render report thereon as required under
Section 124, NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I and to regularly
monitor the progress of the inventory taking;
b. Require the Committee, the General Services Office and the
Municipal Accounting Office to reconcile all the inventory reports
with the books of accounts; and
c. Require the designated Property Officer to maintain property cards
and update them periodically to aid in the conduct of physical count
and to solve or minimize the perennial of unreconciled Accounting
and Property records.

We recommended that Management revert any unutilized amount of
LDRRMF after five years pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 on the
accounting and reporting guidelines for the LDRRMF which shall be used for
other social services to reflect valid and accurate account balances as of
December 31, 2017

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to ensure that withholding taxes are deducted from payments made
in accordance with the Revised Withholding Tax Table. .

We recommended that management exhaust possible means to document the
legality of ownership of the lots through a Transfer Certificate of Title (TCTs) in
the name of the Municipality to protect the interest of the government. We also
recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Assessor give
priority in determining the acquisition costs of the other lots and reconcile with
the accounting records.

We recommended that Management stop granting of honoraria other than
the OSCA Head in compliance with the aforecited regulations of RA 9994.

We recommended that management submit the above-mentioned
documentary requirements for the payment of BAC honoraria pursuant to
Section 5.7.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and section 4(6)
of PD 1445 in order to avoid issuance of Notice of Suspension and/or
Disallowance.

We recommended that management require the Bids and Awards
Committee to comply with the provisions required in the Revised Implementing
Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act No. 9184.
We further recommended the BAC furnish the Audit Team copy of the said
report in order for us to verify/validate the status of the procurement process and
to be used as basis for our contract review on the procured goods and
infrastructure projects.

We recommended that Management include separate caption in the
LDRRMFIP showing the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended
LDRRMF of previous years in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommend that management:
a. Create an Inventory Committee and require the same to conduct physical inventory and render report thereon as required under Section 124, NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I and to regularly monitor the progress of the inventory taking;
b. Require the Inventory Committee, the General Services Office and the Municipal Accounting Office to reconcile all the inventory reports with the books of accounts; and
c. Require the designated Property Officer to maintain property cards and update them periodically to aid in the conduct of physical count and to solve or minimize the perennial of unreconciled Accounting and Property records.

We recommend that management:
a) File a request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts and fund transfers to the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/or Supervising Auditor (SA) pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 in order to present fairly the financial statements of the Municipality.
b) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare aging of dormant receivables, unliquidated cash advances and fund transfers on a quarterly basis to facilitate verification and monitoring of the accounts; and
c) Exert extra effort to locate the whereabouts of the accountable officers and cause the liquidation of the long outstanding cash advances in compliance with the government existing rules and regulations.

We have recommended that management:
Create an Inventory Committee and require the same to conduct physical inventory and render report thereon as required under Section 124, NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I and to regularly monitor the progress of the inventory taking;
Require the Inventory Committee, the General Services Office and the Municipal Accounting Office to reconcile all the inventory reports with the books of accounts; and
Require the designated Property Officer to maintain property cards and update them periodically to aid in the conduct of physical count and to solve or minimize the perennial of unreconciled Accounting and Property records.

We have recommended that management:
File a request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts and fund transfers to the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/or Supervising Auditor (SA) pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 in order to present fairly the financial statements of the Municipality.
Instruct the  Municipal Accountant to prepare aging of dormant receivables, unliquidated cash advances and fund transfers on a quarterly basis to facilitate verification and monitoring of the accounts; and
Exert extra effort to locate the whereabouts of the accountable officers and cause the liquidation of the long outstanding cash advances in compliance with the government existing rules and regulations.

We have recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to the payees of the stale checks. Also, direct the Municipal Accountant to draw a journal entry voucher to take up the cancellation of the stale checks and to remove as reconciling items from the bank reconciliation statements.  Likewise, strictly observe Section 59 of NGAS Manual on LGUs on proper treatment and accounting procedures in recording the transactions of the Municipality in order to have an accurate financial statements.

We have recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to correct the entries made and that proper accounts should be taken up in the books of accounts in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-009, prescribing the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units in order to have an accurate financial statements.
We have recommended that Management strictly adhere with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 in the granting of Cash Advances in order to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit.

We have recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Intra-agency accounts, Due to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds to promptly identify any discrepancy in the balances of the two accounts and to be able to effect the necessary adjustments if appropriate to bring the affected accounts into agreement.

We have recommended that Management stop utilizing Special Education Fund (SEF) to expenditures of telephone and internet to elementary school as it is not one of the priority activities provided in Section 100(c) of Republic Act (RA) 7160.

We have recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain special account in the general fund and consequently prepare separate trial balances and financial statements reflecting the financial condition, results of operations and cash flows, in accordance with the PPSAS and existing policies on the afore-cited provisions of MNGAS for Local Government Units, Vol. I.

We have recommended that the Municipal Engineer and the BAC Chairman furnish copies of perfected contract and each of all the documents forming part thereof to the Office of the Auditor within five (5) working days upon perfection of the contract for review and evaluation thereof.

We have recommended that management open and utilize the Trading Post as center for business of agriculture produce goods in order to assist farmers in the marketing of their products to augment their family income and improve their quality of life and in order to achieve efficiently and effectively the purpose for which the same had been constructed.

We recommend the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Report on the Receipts and Utilization of the Local Risk Reduction and Management Fund and the LDRRMO to submit the report to COA Audit Team as required in COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant effect adjusting entries for the reclassification of the account as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-009 to present fair presentation of the Other Payables; Due to GOCCs and Due to NGAs accounts in the Year-End Financial Statements for CY 2015.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer immediately cause the remittance of the unutilized fund transfer with an aggregate amount of ₱35,189.29 to the corresponding National Government Agencies (NGAs) in full compliance with paragraph 80. (d) of PPSAS, Vol. 1.

We recommend the Municipal Accountant to provide detailed information as to the number and date of issuances of the checks presented as outstanding checks in the BRS to determine whether those checks are still negotiable or already stale checks in compliance with the aforecited regulations; Draw journal entry to record all valid reconciling items that require adjustment and correction of the GL.
